document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sale (Korea)
MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices (UK)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 4 clinical evaluation report for the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation, conformity assessment, benefit-risk assessment, equivalence justification, PMS/PMCF update, and safety/performance evidence for initial CE marking.
keyPoints: The report evaluates conformity with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8 for safety, performance, benefit-risk ratio, and undesirable side-effects.
The device is a wired or wireless indirect digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
The device is classified as Class IIb under Rule 10 of Annex VIII to Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The report states the device is undergoing initial CE-marking and was not placed on the market yet in the device description section.
Equivalence is discussed against DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 CE-marked devices.
The report concludes that benefit-risk is acceptable and no serious side effects were found in similar devices.

evidence.evidenceText: 4 December 31, 2024 Revision according to PMS update

evidence.claim: The evaluated product is the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.

evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer Name: H&abyz Co., Ltd. Address: 2-Dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The report was prepared in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex XIV.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation addresses MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8.
evidence.evidenceText: we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1 ... General Safety and Performance Requirement 2 ... and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8

evidence.claim: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is undergoing initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The device is not placed on the market yet according to the device description section.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is not placed on the market yet.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient populations Adult and children except infants and pregnant women
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Patient Population

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for mammography, infant and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, and angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is reusable.
evidence.evidenceText: Single use / reusable Reusable

evidence.claim: The report states that the device has no known side effects.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Side Effects

evidence.claim: The device contacts intact skin and the patient contacts the detector front for less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: It contacts intact skin surfaces only. Transient Y Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIb under MDR Annex VIII Rule 10.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The device uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: The detector supports wired or wireless operation and auto exposure detection.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology

evidence.claim: The device service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Service Life

evidence.claim: Equivalence was claimed against DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31.
evidence.evidenceText: We indicated DRX-1 and CXDI-70C and CXDI-31 as equivalence products.

evidence.claim: DRX-1 is identified as a Carestream Health CE-marked device by NB 0086.
evidence.evidenceText: DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086)

evidence.claim: CXDI-70C and CXDI-31 are identified as Canon CE-marked devices by NB 0197.
evidence.evidenceText: CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197) CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

evidence.claim: The benchmark previous generation device is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: The benchmark device, which is previous generations of the device of A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW, is ADD.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: Clinical imaging was obtained for 30 sites and evaluated by a qualified clinical evaluator.
evidence.evidenceText: In addition, clinical images were acquired for 30 sites and evaluated by a qualified Clinical Evaluator.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the benefits of the device outweigh the risks.
evidence.evidenceText: We have confirmed that the expected clinical benefit of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector ... can be achieved through clinical data. The benefits of our device outweigh the risks.

evidence.claim: The PMS and PMCF update covers May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 1st Information Collection Period: May 06, 2024(Initial CE marking) ~ December 31, 2024

evidence.claim: The 2024 PMS update reports zero customer feedback incidents and zero incident feedback report cases.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer feedback Years Device Function Packaging Incident 2024 0 0 0 0 ... Incident feedback Years Report Cases MFDS FDA MHRA 2024 0 0 0

evidence.claim: The next clinical evaluation update frequency is every year.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every year.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata signals FDA, but the document content is primarily MDR/CE clinical evaluation and only uses FDA as one recall-data source.

Market status appears internally inconsistent: the device description says the product is not placed on the market yet, while the PMS update lists 2024 sales in Korea, United States of America, India, UK, and Thailand.
A/D conversion evidence conflicts: the technical specification states 16 bits, while the equivalence table lists the device under clinical evaluation as 14 bits.

Classification evidence is internally inconsistent: the criteria table marks 'Device intended for recording of X-ray diagnostic images?' as 'N', but the report classifies the device as Class IIb under Rule 10 for devices specifically intended for recording diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.
Some OCR or source quality issues are present, including duplicated wording, apparent typos such as 'MDCC 2020-5', and inconsistent standard references such as EN 62222-1-1 versus EN 62220-1-1.
